monetary_values.description: Monthly remote work stipend for internet, electricity, and heating
Maximum travel reimbursement for unscheduled office visits with less than 48 hours' notice

locations.context: Employee home address in acknowledgment section

key_facts.fact: Betriebsrat approval obtained June 12, 2025 per Section 97(1)(2) ArbVG
Policy applies to all employees across headquarters in Graz, Linz engineering office, and Munich representative office
Employees must have completed a minimum 6-month probation period to be eligible for remote work
Two employees have overdue background checks: AT-EMP-0156 (DI Robert Steiner) and AT-EMP-0189 (Ing. Lisa Mayr)
Remote-eligible employees must work from office minimum 2 days per week (Anchor Days)
Core Hours are Monday through Friday, 09:30 to 15:30 CET
Full-time contractual weekly hours are 38.5 hours per week per the Kollektivvertrag for metal and technology industry
Security incidents must be reported within 1 hour via hotline +43 316 873 5599 (24/7)
All Company data must remain within the EEA at all times
One-time home office setup allowance is up to EUR 750.00
Monthly remote work stipend is EUR 60.00, tax-exempt up to EUR 3.00 per day (max EUR 300/year)
Whistleblower report WB-2025-0014 alleges falsified time tracking in Engineering Consulting division affecting Siemens Austria billing (project SA-2025-PMI)
Approved exception: Ing. Katharina Berger (AT-EMP-0091) permanent remote work due to mobility impairment from workplace accident, AUVA case GZ-2024-31847
Approved exception: DI Markus Novak (AT-EMP-0134) temporary remote work for ongoing chemotherapy, medical certificate ref MED-2025-4192
PII in acknowledgment section: Sandra Koller SV-Nr 4827 120388, DOB 12.03.1988, home address, personal email sandra.koller@gmx.at

structured_data.approved_by: Mag. Christina Wieser, Head of People & Organization
eligibility_rules: Completed probation period (minimum 6 months per Kollektivvertrag)
Performance rating of 'Meets Expectations' or higher
Role does not require continuous physical presence (determined by department head with Betriebsrat consultation)
Suitable home office environment meeting Section 3 requirements
Completed mandatory 'Secure Remote Work for Engineers' training module
Background check (Strafregisterbescheinigung) renewed within last 24 months
excluded_roles: On-site commissioning and field service engineers during active deployment phases
Lab technicians and test facility operators
Employees in first 6 months of employment (probation period)

hybrid_requirements.anchor_day_policy: One common Anchor Day per team designated by department head; second chosen by employee in coordination with manager

company_provided: Laptop computer (standard Company-issued ThinkPad or equivalent)
Headset with microphone for video conferencing
VPN hardware token for secure network access
Company mobile phone (for engineers with on-call responsibilities)
employee_responsibility: Ergonomic desk and chair (see expense reimbursement)
Dedicated quiet workspace with closable door
Internet connection (minimum 50 Mbps down / 10 Mbps up)

working_hours.camera_policy: On during video meetings unless bandwidth constraints prevent it

security_requirements.description: All work exclusively on Company-issued devices. Personal devices prohibited for any work activities including email.
Company-provided VPN must be active at all times when accessing Company systems.
Must remain enabled on all devices at all times.
Auto-engage after maximum 3 minutes of inactivity.
Confidential data must reside on Company cloud storage (Azure Blob Storage, EU geo-redundancy).
Printing of confidential documents at home strictly prohibited.
Report security incidents within 1 hour to IT Security hotline: +43 316 873 5599 (24/7). GDPR Art. 33 reporting within 72 hours.
All Company data must remain within the EEA. Remote work outside EEA requires DPO approval, limited to 10 business days per year.

expense_limits.setup_allowance_covers: Ergonomic desk, office chair, and workspace essentials
non_reimbursable: Co-working space memberships (unless pre-approved by department head)

manager_obligations: Clear, measurable objectives with defined timelines
Prompt addressing of performance concerns with documentation
employee_obligations: Proactive communication of progress and blockers
Regular status updates in team channels
Active participation in team meetings and Company events
Completion of mandatory training and professional development
performance_decline_consequences: Mandatory return to full-time on-site work for minimum 90 days
Disciplinary action up to and including termination (subject to ArbVG and Betriebsrat consultation)

examples: Extended remote work from outside the EEA

approval_chain.condition: Only for exceptions involving data security

escalation_channels: Works Council (Betriebsrat) involvement per Section 97 ArbVG
exceptions.emergency_provisions: Company may mandate remote work for all employees in public health emergencies, natural disasters, or extraordinary circumstances. Communicated by Managing Director (Geschäftsführer) in consultation with Betriebsrat.

revision_history.author: DI Robert Steiner
revision_history.changes: Added data sovereignty requirements (Section 5.5), DACH provisions

revision_history.author: Mag. Christina Wieser
revision_history.changes: Updated expense amounts per Homeoffice-Pauschale, revised eligibility criteria, added emergency provisions

revision_history.author: Mag. Christina Wieser
revision_history.changes: Clarified Core Hours per AZG, updated security audit provisions, added escalation procedures with Betriebsrat

summary: Remote work policy (POL-HR-2025-012, v1.3) for AlpenTech GmbH (Graz), effective July 1, 2025, last revised September 15, 2025. Approved by Mag. Christina Wieser with Betriebsrat consultation. Eligible employees (6+ months tenure, satisfactory performance, completed security training, current background check) may work remotely with minimum 2 office days per week. Core Hours are 09:30-15:30 CET with 30-minute response time. Strict data security requirements include mandatory VPN, Company-device-only usage, no local data storage (Azure Blob Storage EU), no home printing, and 1-hour security incident reporting to +43 316 873 5599. All data must remain within the EEA. Contains sensitive PII including SV-Nr, home addresses, personal emails, DOBs, medical information (chemotherapy treatment, mobility impairment), employee IDs, and whistleblower investigation details (WB-2025-0014, alleging falsified time tracking for Siemens Austria billing).
